entities.name: NUTRIENT DEFICIENCY
entities.description: A condition where the body does not receive enough essential nutrients, threatening nutritional security

entities.name: NUTRITIONAL SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: MALNUTRITION
entities.description: A condition resulting from an unbalanced diet, including undernutrition and overnutrition

entities.name: NON-COMMUNICABLE DISEASES
entities.description: Chronic diseases that are not passed from person to person, often linked to poor nutrition

entities.name: CEREALS
entities.description: Grain-bearing grasses used as a staple food source

entities.name: LEGUMES
entities.description: Protein-rich plants of the Fabaceae family widely grown in sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: A geographical region south of the Sahara desert

entities.name: SOUTH ASIA
entities.description: A geographical region in the southern part of Asia

entities.name: GENETIC VARIATION
entities.description: The diversity in genetic information among individuals of a species, existing in the germplasm pool

entities.name: NUTRITIONAL TRAITS
entities.description: Heritable characteristics that determine the nutrient composition of a plant

entities.name: GERMPLASM POOL
entities.description: The total genetic resources available for a particular crop species

entities.name: SELECTIVE BREEDING
entities.description: A method of breeding plants to enhance desired traits by utilizing genetic variation

entities.name: BREEDING PROGRAM
entities.description: A systematic approach to improving crop varieties through genetic selection to mitigate malnutrition

entities.name: MOLECULAR MECHANISMS
entities.description: The biological processes at the molecular level that govern nutrient acquisition, transport, and metabolism

entities.name: NUTRIENT ACQUISITION
entities.description: The process by which plants obtain nutrients from their environment

entities.name: TRANSPORT
entities.description: The movement of nutrients from the site of acquisition to storage or use within the plant

entities.name: METABOLISM
entities.description: The chemical reactions in a cell that convert nutrients into energy and building blocks

entities.name: BIOFORTIFICATION STRATEGY
entities.description: A method to increase the nutritional value of food crops through breeding or biotechnology

entities.name: NUTRIENT CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of essential nutrients within a plant part
